description: Add first-class descriptor query capability metadata to the compiled MappingSet, separate from the existing root-table RelationalQueryCapability. Include supported and omitted states, supported descriptor fields, stable omission diagnostics, and a lookup helper such as GetDescriptorQueryCapabilityOrThrow. Acceptance: descriptor resources can be resolved through descriptor-specific capability metadata; non-descriptor RelationalQueryCapability behavior remains unchanged; descriptor resources still remain intentionally omitted from the generic root-table query path; omitted descriptor capabilities produce an actionable message suitable for HTTP 501 responses; missing descriptor capability metadata for a SharedDescriptorTable resource is treated as a deterministic internal guard-rail failure.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for descriptor query capability lookup, missing metadata guard rails, supported vs omitted state, and omitted-resource diagnostics.

category: Descriptor Query Capability Compilation and Manifest
description: Compile descriptor query capability entries during MappingSet compilation from ConcreteResourceModel.DescriptorMetadata.ColumnContract and expose them in mapping-set contract/manifest output. Support descriptor query fields id, namespace, codeValue, shortDescription, description, effectiveBeginDate, and effectiveEndDate. Treat DescriptorMetadata.ColumnContract as authoritative and add the cheap ApiSchema queryFieldMapping sanity check: if the schema disagrees for a descriptor resource, mark only that descriptor capability omitted with a clear diagnostic instead of failing the whole MappingSet. Acceptance: every SharedDescriptorTable resource receives descriptor query capability metadata; query parameter names are case-insensitive; supported field-to-column mappings are deterministic; generic RelationalQueryCapability still reports descriptor resources as omitted with the existing descriptor-path reason; mapping-set manifests/goldens expose descriptor query capability diagnostics without changing non-descriptor query capability output.
steps-to-verify: Add compiler tests covering supported descriptor fields, case-insensitive query field names, resource-scoped omission on ApiSchema mismatch, and continued generic query omission for SharedDescriptorTable resources.
Update mapping-set manifest/golden tests to include descriptor query capability metadata and omission diagnostics.

description: Carry AuthorizationStrategyEvaluators through IGetRequest, GetRequest, and RelationalGetRequest so relational descriptor GET can fail closed without invoking the legacy ResourceAuthorizationHandler. Populate the evaluators from GetByIdHandler and preserve the stored-document internal read path used by profile write validation. Acceptance: descriptor GET allows empty strategies or only NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired; any other effective strategy returns GetFailureNotImplemented with a diagnostic naming the resource and strategies; relational descriptor GET does not call the legacy authorization handler; existing non-descriptor GET behavior remains unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Add Core/backend unit tests for evaluator propagation from GetByIdHandler and for descriptor GET fail-closed behavior.

category: Descriptor Read Handler Registration and Resource Detection
description: Introduce IDescriptorReadHandler/DescriptorReadHandler as a DI-registered relational read component parallel to IDescriptorWriteHandler, inject it into RelationalDocumentStoreRepository, and make descriptor read routing use MappingSet.TryGetDescriptorResourceModel(resource, out descriptorModel) as the source of truth rather than ResourceInfo.IsDescriptor alone. Delegate descriptor GET-by-id and descriptor QueryDocuments before generic read-plan or generic GetQueryCapabilityOrThrow paths. Acceptance: DI resolves the repository with the descriptor read handler registered; SharedDescriptorTable resources route to IDescriptorReadHandler even when caller metadata is incomplete; non-descriptor resources continue through the existing generic relational read/query paths; descriptor writes and deletes continue to use IDescriptorWriteHandler unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Add service registration tests showing IDescriptorReadHandler is registered with the relational backend services.
Add repository unit tests proving descriptor GET/query delegation is based on MappingSet SharedDescriptorTable metadata and non-descriptor GET/query still use the generic paths.

category: Descriptor Read Row Contracts and SQL Readers
description: Add descriptor read row contracts and provider-neutral reader helpers for rows returned from dms.Document plus dms.Descriptor. The row shape must include DocumentId, DocumentUuid, ContentLastModifiedAt, ResourceKeyId, Namespace, CodeValue, ShortDescription, Description, EffectiveBeginDate, EffectiveEndDate, and optional diagnostic Discriminator when selected. Acceptance: required descriptor fields namespace, codeValue, and shortDescription being null are classified as invariant/corruption failures; optional null fields are represented as null for later omission; date values round-trip as DateOnly; row readers do not depend on per-descriptor physical tables or project-schema hydration.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for row-reader null handling, DateOnly conversion, required-field invariant failures, optional-field null preservation, and provider column ordinal/name stability.

description: Implement descriptor GET-by-id in IDescriptorReadHandler. The handler must read only dms.Document plus dms.Descriptor, resolve DocumentUuid to DocumentId, scope by the expected ResourceKeyId, and treat dms.Document.ResourceKeyId as authoritative while leaving dms.Descriptor.Discriminator diagnostic-only. Acceptance: missing UUID or UUID for the wrong resource returns GetFailureNotExists; matching dms.Document with no dms.Descriptor row returns an UnknownFailure invariant error; a mismatched descriptor Discriminator does not change resource discrimination; no per-descriptor tables, IDocumentHydrator, IRelationalReadMaterializer, or project-schema reconstitution are used; PostgreSQL and SQL Server SQL are parameterized and dialect-quoted consistently.
steps-to-verify: Add repository/handler unit tests for successful descriptor GET delegation, wrong-resource 404, missing descriptor-row invariant failure, diagnostic-only Discriminator mismatch, and no generic hydrator/materializer invocation for descriptor resources.

description: Implement descriptor row-to-JSON materialization for both RelationalGetRequestReadMode values. ExternalResponse emits namespace, codeValue, shortDescription, description, effectiveBeginDate, effectiveEndDate plus id, _etag, and _lastModifiedDate; StoredDocument emits only the public descriptor fields. Optional null fields are omitted, required fields are present, dates use yyyy-MM-dd, _lastModifiedDate uses the stored dms.Document ContentLastModifiedAt in UTC second precision, and _etag is base64(SHA-256(canonical served JSON)). Acceptance: Uri, Discriminator, and ChangeVersion are never emitted; _etag changes after readable profile projection; StoredDocument has no metadata and no profile projection.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for null omission, date formatting, metadata injection, StoredDocument mode, exclusion of Uri/Discriminator/ChangeVersion, and _etag recomputation after projection.

description: Wire readable profile projection for descriptor GET/query responses while preserving descriptor identity fields. Core should provide descriptor identity property names namespace and codeValue in addition to the existing server-generated fields id, _etag, and _lastModifiedDate; the descriptor read handler should invoke the existing IReadableProfileProjector only for ExternalResponse mode and recompute _etag after projection. Acceptance: namespace and codeValue survive readable profiles even when descriptor identity is synthetic; id, _etag, and _lastModifiedDate survive all readable projections; StoredDocument reads remain unprojected; generic non-descriptor profile projection behavior is unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for descriptor readable profile projection preserving namespace/codeValue and API metadata while filtering non-identity descriptor fields.
Add Core seam tests showing descriptor read requests pass namespace/codeValue as identity property names when a readable profile applies.

description: Implement descriptor-specific query preprocessing and dialect SQL planning from descriptor query capabilities. Support exact-match predicates for id, namespace, codeValue, shortDescription, description, effectiveBeginDate, and effectiveEndDate; parse id as UUID with invalid values returning an empty page; preserve DMS-993 exact-match semantics for date values; use case-sensitive/ordinal matching for string values; page deterministically by dms.Document.DocumentId ascending and optionally compile totalCount SQL. Acceptance: query parameter lookup is case-insensitive, descriptor string values are not normalized, ?namespace=<namespace>#<codeValue> only matches when that full value is stored in Namespace, SQL never targets per-descriptor tables, generated parameter names are stable across PostgreSQL and SQL Server.
steps-to-verify: Add unit/golden tests for PostgreSQL and SQL Server descriptor query SQL, stable parameter names, id empty-page behavior, all supported public fields, totalCount SQL, strict namespace matching, and no per-descriptor table references.

category: Descriptor Query Delegation and Guard Rails
description: Add descriptor GET-many entry-point handling to RelationalDocumentStoreRepository and IDescriptorReadHandler before the generic GetQueryCapabilityOrThrow path. Use descriptor query capability lookup, enforce ResourceKeyId discrimination, fail closed for non-no-op authorization, and return an empty QuerySuccess for empty-page preprocessing. Acceptance: omitted descriptor capability returns QueryFailureNotImplemented with the descriptor capability diagnostic; unsupported authorization returns QueryFailureNotImplemented with the resource and strategy names; invalid id preprocessing returns an empty page without executing SQL; generic IDocumentHydrator and project-schema reconstitution are not invoked for descriptor resources.
steps-to-verify: Add repository/handler unit tests for descriptor query delegation, omitted capability 501, auth fail-closed 501, empty-page preprocessing, and no generic hydrator/materializer invocation.

category: Descriptor Query Row Retrieval and Page Materialization
description: Execute descriptor GET-many page and total-count SQL through IDescriptorReadHandler against dms.Document plus dms.Descriptor, then materialize the page directly from descriptor rows. The selected page must be based on dms.Document rows scoped by ResourceKeyId, and descriptor rows must be validated after selection so corruption is not silently inner-joined away. Acceptance: totalCount=true is honored; returned items are ordered by DocumentId; selected descriptor documents missing dms.Descriptor rows return a 500-style invariant failure; materialization applies readable profile projection for ExternalResponse mode and recomputes _etag per served item; no per-descriptor physical tables are used.
steps-to-verify: Add handler unit tests for totalCount, ordered materialization, selected-document missing descriptor-row invariant failure, profile-projected query items, and provider-specific command shape.

description: Add shared PostgreSQL and SQL Server integration-test helpers for directly seeding dms.Document and dms.Descriptor rows and for resolving descriptor ResourceKeyId values from the MappingSet. Keep these helpers scoped to descriptor read tests and avoid depending on descriptor POST unless a test explicitly covers write/read integration. Acceptance: both providers can seed valid descriptor rows, wrong-resource document rows, and corrupt missing-descriptor-row cases deterministically; helpers preserve dms schema metadata; tests can request ExternalResponse and StoredDocument modes through RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.
steps-to-verify: Add provider helper tests or smoke tests proving seeded rows can be read by raw SQL before repository assertions run.

category: Provider Integration GET and Invariant Tests
description: Add PostgreSQL and SQL Server backend integration tests for descriptor GET behavior using direct dms.Document/dms.Descriptor seeding. Cover GET by id, StoredDocument mode, wrong-resource 404, diagnostic-only Discriminator mismatch, selected-document missing descriptor-row invariant failure, null optional-field omission, metadata shape, _lastModifiedDate, and exclusion of Uri/Discriminator/ChangeVersion. Acceptance: both providers pass the same GET/invariant matrix and prove descriptor reads use the shared descriptor table rather than per-descriptor physical tables.

category: Provider Integration Query Filter and Paging Tests
description: Add PostgreSQL and SQL Server backend integration tests for descriptor query behavior using direct dms.Document/dms.Descriptor seeding. Cover query filters for id, namespace, codeValue, shortDescription, description, effectiveBeginDate, and effectiveEndDate; totalCount=true; strict namespace matching; ordered results; and paging across at least two pages. Acceptance: both providers pass the same query matrix; descriptor query SQL is provider-correct for UUID/date/paging/count behavior; tests prove ?namespace=<namespace>#<codeValue> only matches when that full value is stored in Namespace.

Enable or add a thin relational-backend E2E scenario that creates a descriptor through POST, reads it by id, queries it by descriptor fields, and exercises readable profile projection through the public API. Prefer tagging existing descriptor GET/query scenarios with relational-backend when their expectations already match the relational design; add a focused scenario only where legacy expectations conflict. Acceptance: the scenario proves Core routing, descriptor write/read integration, descriptor query routing, fail-closed authorization behavior where applicable, profile projection, _etag recomputation, _lastModifiedDate, and correct public descriptor shape without Uri or Discriminator; deeper provider/filter/invariant coverage remains in backend integration tests.

Run the final formatting and regression sweep for DMS-994 after the implementation tasks land. Acceptance: modified C# files are csharpier-formatted without unrelated directory-wide churn, descriptor read/query unit and integration tests pass, generic relational query/read tests still pass, and any intentional legacy descriptor-query expectation changes are documented in test names or comments.
steps-to-verify: Run: dotnet csharpier format <modified C# files or the smallest modified directories>
Run the PostgreSQL and SQL Server descriptor read integration tests from the provider integration tasks.
Run the relational descriptor E2E filter from the Relational Descriptor API E2E task.
